description: Contains milk proteins that can trigger allergic reactions.
Dried milk containing allergenic milk proteins.
Whey is a milk protein that may cause allergic reactions.
Casein is the primary protein found in milk and is a common allergen.
Paneer is a dairy product made from milk and contains milk proteins.
Ghee contains very low milk proteins but may still affect highly sensitive individuals.
Soy proteins can trigger allergic reactions in people with soy allergy.
Soy lecithin is an emulsifier usually derived from soy and may affect sensitive individuals.
Soy protein is a concentrated soy ingredient that can cause allergic reactions.
Wheat contains proteins that may trigger wheat allergies.
Wheat flour contains gluten which is unsafe for people with gluten-related disorders.
Maida is refined wheat flour and contains gluten.
Atta is whole wheat flour and naturally contains gluten.
Suji or semolina is made from wheat and contains gluten.
Rava is derived from wheat and contains gluten.
Egg proteins can cause allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.
Peanuts are one of the most common causes of severe food allergies.
Groundnut is another name for peanut and can trigger severe allergic reactions.
Almond is a tree nut that may cause allergic reactions.
Cashew is a tree nut that can trigger allergic reactions.
Sesame seeds are a common food allergen and may cause severe reactions.
Fish proteins can cause allergic reactions even after cooking.
Shrimp is a shellfish that commonly causes severe allergic reactions.
Sugar is generally considered safe and is not a common food allergen.
Salt is generally safe and is not associated with food allergies.
